methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables in Defense & Security, supplemented by a broader universe of 10 public and private frontier-tech peers in the sector.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, implying $12M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $300M, with a range spanning $60M (Tenna Systems at Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI at Series A). The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in stage and market position within the Defense & Security vertical.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $11M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $156M. The low-end estimate is anchored by stage-progression methodology and proximity to current fundraising. The high end reflects peer medians including more mature Series A comparables. Key uncertainties include Deepnight's product differentiation relative to Tenna Systems (the only true Seed-stage peer), the company's revenue traction relative to Series A benchmarks, and sector-specific customer concentration or regulatory risk. A valuation in the $25M–$75M range may be more defensible pending clarity on product-market fit and near-term fundraising milestones.
